title: Should We Pull the Plug?
description: A teammate is reluctant to kill a project that already cost six months. Help them see the sunk-cost trap.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: We've spent six months on this. We can't just kill it now.

templates.text: I get it. But that money is already gone, no matter what we choose next.
templates.hint_zh: 我懂。但那筆錢無論我們現在怎麼選,都回不來了。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: So you're saying ignore the cost?

templates.text: For the decision, yes. The only question is whether the next month is worth it.
templates.hint_zh: 在決策上,對。唯一該問的是接下來那個月值不值得。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: But if we ship something, it's at least a return on the investment.

templates.text: Only if the shipped thing has real users. Otherwise we're just doubling down on a loss.
templates.hint_zh: 前提是出貨的東西真的有人用。不然只是加碼追虧損。

templates.text: If five users today say they'd pay for the v1 feature set, we keep going.
templates.hint_zh: 如果今天有五個用戶願意為 v1 功能付費,我們就繼續。

templates.text: Then we shut it down on Friday and move the team. Six months is a lesson, not a contract.
templates.hint_zh: 週五關掉,團隊轉移。六個月是學費,不是合約。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Painful but fair. Let me run the user check today.

templates.text: Thanks. Whatever you find, we'll act on it.
templates.hint_zh: 謝。不管你查到什麼,我們就照那個走。
